observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the MAccO and the Inventory Committee reconcile the difference between the PPE account balances recorded in the books and in the RPCPPE on a per account basis to ensure the existence, accuracy and completeness of such PPE account balances in the financial statements as at year-end.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of the costs of the completed projects from CIP accounts to Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets accounts upon submission by the Municipal Engineer of the required supporting documents;
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to institutionalize the recognition of the corresponding CIP and Accounts Payable balances representing the Final Progress Billing to be made by the contractor upon obtaining the Certificate of Completion and immediately transfer the completed projects to its corresponding PPE account; and
The Municipal Engineer to inform the contractors to submit the necessary supporting documents for their claim on the unbilled portion of the completed projects and encourage them to secure complete documents for processing of final payment. Otherwise, outstanding unliquidated accounts payable for two years or more shall be reverted to unappropriated surplus of the GF pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We recommended that:
The Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory and submit to the Audit Team the required RPCI and RPCSP in its prescribed format not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balance of the said account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
Conduct an immediate reconciliation of respective records pertaining to Cash in Local Treasury account with a view to ensuring that the reported balances are accurate, complete, and consistent and make the necessary corrections and/or adjustments thereon, if needed; and
Prospectively, perform regular reconciliation of accounts pursuant to Section 143 of the LTOM, 2nd Edition, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct an immediate reconciliation of respective records pertaining to CIB-LCCA and CIB-LCSA with a view to ensuring that the reported balances are accurate, complete, and consistent and make the necessary corrections and/or adjustments thereon, if needed;
Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer perform regular reconciliation of accounts pursuant to Section 143 of LTOM, 2nd Edition, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer ensure that relevant records are readily available to support the requirements of fiscal or control agencies of the government, as stipulated in the above-cited provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer’s Office to trace and verify the resulting net variance of negative ₱1,545,596.13 and ₱1,800,999.18 between the balances presented in the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and its reciprocal accounts in the PGCN;
Prepare the necessary journal entries to reconcile and reflect the correct/adjusted Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs account balances, if needed; and
Henceforth, conduct periodic verification, analysis and validation of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts against the records of all other concerned LGUs.

We recommended that Management initiate the actual disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with existing rules and regulations, with a view of effecting necessary adjustments in the books to correct the overstatement in PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the MAccO and GSO:
Ensure that the RPCPPE reflects comprehensively and cumulatively the PPE of the Municipality as of the date of the conduct of physical count;

Ensure that adjustments for identified differences arising from the reconciliation of accounting records and the RPCPPE be made promptly and properly.

We recommended that the LCE:
Prospectively, ensure that the required prior assessment on the indigency of the deceased or financial capability of the nearest kin be done by the LSWDO before the Municipality shall bear the cost of burial. Moreover, in relation to Transaction No. 3, submit the documents relative to the assessments conducted by the LSWDO;
Re-evaluate/revisit its MOA with the designated funeral service provider and consider aligning the same with the applicable provisions of existing rules and regulations cited herein; and
To support the accuracy, validity and propriety of the claims, in case the cost of burial shall be borne by the LGU, include duly signed documents showing the acknowledgment from the kin/bereaved family of the cost of burial accorded to their deceased relative.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records at least quarterly. Take up immediately necessary corrections in books requiring adjustments to reconcile both cashbook and the ledger.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer ensure that Cashbooks maintained by the AOs conform strictly with the prescribed format and instructions provided in the LTOM, such that the required columns are appropriately and completely filled-up. Likewise, ensure that the required AO’s certification at the end of each month in the Collector’s Cashbooks are complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Evaluate each item of PPE noted in this observation against the recognition criteria under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSASs) taking into consideration:
The capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00; and
The exercise of judgment, when warranted (such that when it may be appropriate to aggregate individually insignificant items, such as library books, computer peripherals, and small items of equipment, and to apply the criteria to the aggregate value).
When warranted, after the evaluation, effect the necessary and appropriate reclassification or adjusting entry in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that LCE:
Require the concerned offices and General Services Section to consolidate and submit the report of distribution to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books;
Based on the RSMI and Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) submitted, the Municipal accountant derecognize in the books the cost of inventories which were already issued and distributed to end-users to ensure proper accounting; and
All offices concerned, observed strict compliance with the guidelines set on internal control and systematic inventory management for the proper recognition of inventory and expenses to ensure that inventory balances are fairly presented in the FS in accordance with IPSASs 12.
We recommended that the LCE and SB shall see to it that the annual and supplemental appropriations shall be compliant with all the requirements set forth in RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE and SB ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances strictly observe and comply with the applicable rules and regulations in the process of budgeting so as not to hamper delivery of vital public services.

We recommended that the LCE and SB ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances strictly observe and comply with the applicable rules and regulations in the process of budgeting so as not to hamper the delivery of vital public services.

Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant and other Offices concerned to determine the causes of the non-settlement of the payables that are outstanding for more than a year and address the same.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the BAC to strictly observe Annex H of R-IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Submit valid justification on the BAC’s delay on the issuance of PO after the recommendation of the HOPE to award the contract.

We recommended that the BAC adhere strictly with the required documentations on adoption of Alternative Modes of Procurement, such as Small Value Procurement, in the procurement of goods as provided in the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE and SB ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances strictly observe and comply with the applicable rules and regulations in the process of budgeting so as not to hamper delivery of public service including those gender issues/gaps responsive projects/activities identified for funding supported by sex disaggregated data for the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations for Management to:
Ensure that the RPCPPE reflects comprehensively and cumulatively the PPE of the Municipality as of the date of the conduct of physical count;
Designate personnel from the Accounting Office and General Services Office (GSO) dedicated in the reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records as well as upkeep of property records in the respective offices; and
Identified differences must be timely coordinated with the Municipal Accountant and Officer-in-Charge (OIC)-GSO for determination of appropriate adjustments.

We recommended that Management initiate the actual disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with existing rules and regulations, with a view of effecting necessary adjustments in the books to correct the overstatement in PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Fast track the reconciliation and verification of the long outstanding receivables and expedite compliance of all the conditions and requirements provided under COA Circular No.  2016-005 to facilitate submission and approval of request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts, if need be; and
Conduct regular and periodic monitoring and analysis of receivables to evaluate the existence and collectability of the accounts and ensure that the same are collected when due and demandable.

We recommended that Management:
Perform periodic verification, analysis and validation of the Due from LGUs account with concerned LGUs; and
Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct/adjusted Due from LGUs account balances.

We recommended that Management:
Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
Perform constant review of recorded transactions to deter error and omissions.
We recommended that Management monitor and reconcile the Due to BIR account to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and effect necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management:
Perform periodic verification, analysis and validation of the Due to LGUs account with concerned LGUs; and
Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct/adjusted Due to LGUs account balances.

We recommended that Management:
Issue demand letters to:
Notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated CA/s. If settlement was not received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time to liquidate;
Official/s who already ended their term and employees of Department of Education (Teacher) to immediately cause the liquidation or settlement of the unliquidated balances; and
Request the write-off of the unliquidated CAs of the dead, retired or absconded employee/s following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant unliquidated CA in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
For evaluation of the Team;
Submit to the Audit Team a valid justification on the LGU’s inability to secure that blue tags are attached to the 625 sacks of “certified” palay seeds received by the LGU. Likewise, submit the subject blue tags, if practicable. Otherwise, provide equally relevant proof that the 625 sacks of palay seeds were actually “certified”; and
Submit the duly signed list of actual farmer-beneficiaries and the pictures taken during the distribution.
Ensure that, in case of specific fund transfers/grants received (such as the BGCM), the specific guidelines issued corresponding to its utilization (such as LBC No. 125) be faithfully complied and adhered with, thus, securing the achievement of its purpose and the intent of the law where the same emanated, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

We recommended that Management:
Fully implement the unfinished developmental projects targeted under the 20 per cent DF for CY 2020; and
Direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects identified are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure coordination of the concerned units (Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal Budget Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office, including the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office) to facilitate the transfer at year-end of the current year unexpended LDRRMF from the GF to TF books through the prescribed accounting entries. In case when the reconciliation between budget and accounting records (after the transfer at year-end) resulted to a corrected amount which should have been transferred, promptly effect the necessary adjusting entries;
Effect the reversion to the GF of the unutilized funds from CYs 2012, 2013, and 2014 amounting to ₱999, ₱0.750 million, and ₱ 12,180, respectively, totaling, ₱0.763 million which were already more than five years in the TF; and
Regularly evaluate and monitor the unutilized funds relating to the unexpended LDRRMF of the different prior years already transferred in the TF to consider its possible and timely utilization to achieve its very purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMCs within the next five years from the date of transfer.
We recommended that Management ensure the full implementation of all the identified priority projects funded under SEF.
We recommended that LSB follow strictly JC No. 1, series of 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to strictly comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on procurement, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

Fast track the submission of the duly received and signed RDS indicating the names of recipients and their signatures for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, when warranted; and
Ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation to protect the interests of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

Submit valid justification on the BAC’s inability to follow the operational timelines.

We recommended the Management to strictly implement the withholding of 10 per cent retention money on all payments of infrastructure projects to cover the defects that may arise before the final acceptance.
We recommended the Management to direct the BAC to regularly prepare and submit the PMR in accordance with the provisions of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and in the format prescribed by the GPPB.

We recommended the Management to ensure that all approved contracts for projects implemented are supported with a DOLE-approved Construction Safety and Health Program pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended the Management to require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to revert to the General Fund the accounts payable that are outstanding for two years or more, in accordance with the provisions of P.D. No.1445 and DBM-COA JC No. 99-6.

We recommended the Management to:
Require the BAC to perform its duties and functions strictly in accordance with the provisions of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to avoid possible future audit suspensions and disallowances; and
Require the BAC to enhance its review mechanism on documents generated by the said Committee to minimize, if not totally avoid, recurrence of the oversight/errors noted.

We recommended the Management to:
Optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF and implement the programs, activities and projects (PAPs) within the timelines in order to achieve the socio-economic and environmental outcomes, essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives of a desired quality of life for its constituents; and
Direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects identified are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation.

We recommended the Management to:
Make representation with the Source Agency in order to determine appropriate disposition of the fund transfers given the circumstances that hinders implementation of the projects; and
Draw up implementation timelines to serve as guide in monitoring status of the project to effect prompt attainment of program objectives.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the implementing Office in coordination with the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the unused balance for completed projects to its source agencies to fully liquidate and settle their account; and
Monitor the status of projects funded by national agencies and timely coordinate with the source agency to ensure that any grants be settled accordingly.

We recommended the Management to:
Instruct personnel from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to determine the unremitted taxes withheld based on accounting records and remit the same with the BIR; and
Prospectively, tax remittances should be consistently reconciled with taxes withheld to ensure optimal compliance with existing tax rules and regulations.

We recommended the Management through the MDRRMO to:
Enhance monitoring of LDRRMF utilization focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved.

We recommended the Management to revisit Municipal Ordinance No. 300-11 and consider amendment/modification of its pertinent Sections incorporating inputs from the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with a view of complying with the requirements of PPSAS 1.

We recommended that Management require the GSO to consolidate and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant.  Based on the SSMI, a JEV shall be prepared to record the expenditures using the appropriate expenditure accounts to ensure a fair presentation of the balance per books at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management stressed that distribution of inventories is made per office and RIS are compiled at the General Services Section (GSS).
observations.reason: No SSMI together with RIS consolidated and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for preparation of JEVs.

observations.managementAction:
Related observation incorporated in Part II of this report and in AAR CYs 2014 and 2013.





Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.







Management did not make any comment on the recommendation.
observations.reason:
The Municipality was unable to finish 30 projects funded by the 20 per cent LDF for CY 2018.



Lack of manpower assigned in the preparation of plans and inspection of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instructs the Chairman of the Inventory Committee to submit to the Municipal Accountant the correct Inventory of School Building account to effect the transfer of these school buildings to the school recipients thru JEV to avoid overstatement of asset of the municipality at year end.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant stressed that a copy of the transmittal of the inventory of schools totaling ₱1.88 million funded by the LGU was received on April 8, 2016. They will coordinate with the GSO and Office of the Municipal Engineer to determine the school buildings that should be transferred to DepEd.
observations.reason: The supporting documents attached to the inventory are not complete to affect the transfer such as copy of approved program of works, plans, cost of materials and labor and others and number of original journal entry taking up the school building account which are necessary documents in determining if a complete whole building is/are to be transferred or only the cost of the rehabilitation/ improvement/repair.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Investigate the cause of the variance by determining the present status and valuation of the parcels of land as reported by the Office of the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Office of the Municipal Assessor, then effect necessary reconciliation of the data; and
Determine all land sites owned by the Municipal Government of Labo with a view that the same shall be covered with TCTs under its name and a sufficient budget is allocated for the purpose.
We recommended that Management reclassify the portion of loans payable that will mature within one year from balance sheet date from long-term to current liability.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Install a monitoring mechanism that will facilitate notification to payees or holder of checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance; and
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate and determine the appropriate disposition on these transactions such as (a) in case the stale checks are still in the possession of the local treasury, adjustments in the books of the affected accounts or replacement of checks; and (b) in case the stale checks are in the hands of the payee, replacement of new checks upon compliance with the required procedures.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the BAC to require the concerned Contractors to renew/ extend its performance bond and furnish the Auditor a copy thereof; and
Require the BAC and MEO to designate personnel to monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that the contractors are informed and required to renew their performance securities before its expiration.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Advise the BAC/BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain that all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with Lowest Calculated Bid including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical and operational competence of the winning contractors during the bidding and post-qualification evaluation to avoid future suspension/disallowance in audit; and
Impose administrative sanctions to erring personnel.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer and/or Project Inspector or any agency personnel mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the agency to submit to the Audit Team copies of the Results of Test Analysis/Status of Quality Control Test or a valid justification for our review and evaluation why the related disbursement transaction shall not be suspended in audit for inability to comply such legal requirements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to implement a sound and effective monitoring system to ensure contractor’s compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure that the procuring entity will be reimbursed of its expenses in restoration or reconstruction work in case of Structural Defects/Failure occurring during the applicable warranty period.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the BAC to promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection, and observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the prescribed timeframe for review and inspection; and
Impose administrative sanctions to erring LGU personnel.

We recommended that Management procure their FOL operational requirement by bulk in accordance with the requirements of the fore-going GPPB regulations in order to avail volume discounts and other services offered by gas stations.

We recommended that Management require the SWM Board to implement the incentive programs incorporated in the SWMP or make the necessary revision on the SWMP to include appropriate incentive programs that will encourage active participation of the constituents in the LGU’s SWMP.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to formulate the required Three-year Strategic Program and Recommendation of the DepEd Public Schools District Supervisor (PSDS) and Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) prior to the preparation and approval of the Annual LSB Budget.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
The Members of the SB should immediately stop the practice of granting donations to its constituents regardless of the source of fund as it is not among their mandated powers, duties and functions under Section 447 of R.A. No. 7160; and
Prospectively, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2006-002, Section 118 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 447 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF and implement the PPAs within the timelines in order to achieve the socio-economic and environmental outcomes, essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives of a desired quality of life for its constituents;
Adopt a well-defined strategy in order to enhance the present monitoring and review activities during the planning, procurement, contract implementation and program evaluation phase, in order for the planners, implementers as well as evaluators to continuously monitor the timely implementation, so that immediate appropriate and corrective actions could be done on noted gaps along the way; and
Re-appropriate the funds of PPAs which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings.

We recommended that Management hasten the implementation of projects and the utilization of funds thereof in order to deliver the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries. Draw up implementation timelines to serve as guide in monitoring status of the project to effect prompt attainment of program objectives.

We recommended that Management through the MDRRMO:
Enhance monitoring of LDRRMF utilization focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Update/execute lease contracts for the market stall holders and impose the conditions set forth in the said contracts;
Observe the posting of the Notice Time for Collection of Tax and List of Delinquent Taxpayers to each of the 52 Barangays of the Municipality as required by R.A. No. 7160; and
Incorporate additional penalty provisions with regard to unpaid market stall fees, such as cancellation of the lease contract/agreement, in the Municipal Revenue Code to ensure that revenue collections can be effectively enforced.

to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation,
disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment,
based on the source documents of the transactions.

» Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee and appraise the market
value of the unserviceable properties;

> Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the most
appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular
No. 95-006 and COA Circular No. 89-296 which are either through (a)
public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to
other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by
destruction;

» Facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable
properties to recoup any salvage value thereof; and

> Ensure proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further
deterioration.

We recommended that Management negotiate with the lender bank for an
additional or flexible repayment period of the loan incurred in CY 2016 to ensure
that the appropriation for debt servicing shall not exceed 20 per cent of the estimated
regular income of the LGU in the ensuing years, until the loan is paid in order for the
LGU to have available funds for the implementation of other priority programs,
projects and activities.

Henceforth, in future loans to be contracted by the LGU, Management
observe strict compliance with the prescribed limit on appropriating for debt
servicing in the Annual Budget of the LGU.

‘We recommended that Management:

» Comply with the mandatory requirement of law in providing the correct
and appropriate amount for local development projects; and

» Limit the amount allowable and ensure that the appropriation for debt
servicing shall not exceed 20 per cent of the estimated regular income of
the LGU in the ensuing years, until the loan is paid in order for the LGU to
have available funds for the implementation of other priority programs,
projects and activities under the local development fund.

We recommended the Management to strictly observe the provisions of
DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 on the
utilization and prioritization of the SEF that will improve the standard and quality of
education. We also recommend the Management to advise the Local School Board
officials to give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in the cited
law and regulations in the preparation of SEF budget.

We recommended that Management strictly impose the existing regulation
pertaining to the cash advance for payroll fund and adherence with the prescribed
period of rendering the required liquidation.

We recommended that:

» The Municipal Accountant to document the request for write-off for
account balances which were considered dormant and where collectability
could no longer be ascertained pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005;

» The Municipal Mayor to file request for authority to write — off dormant
receivable accounts; and

> Require the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the collection process and
monitoring of the receivables of the municipality to avoid future loss due to
write off of uncollectible accounts.

We recommended that the four mandatory positions in the LDRRMO be filled
up by permanent employees in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC
No. 2014-1.

‘We recommended that management:

> Follow the Calendar Schedule for the preparation of GPB for LGUs and
incorporate the DILG-approved/endorsed GPB in the Annual Budget to
ensure that the programs, projects and activities reflected thereat are in
accordance with the existing regulations and guidelines;

> Submit annual GAD Accomplishment Report accompanied by the
required documents specified in the above-cited provisions;

> Set-up and maintain GAD database as spearheaded by the he LPDO that
will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and
policy formulation; and

> Follow PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC
No. 2016-01 to fully address and implement applicable provisions in the
convention on the elimination of all forms of discrimination against women
and promote gender equality within the municipality.

‘We recommended that Management:

> Commit to the adaptation of controlled dumpsite by meeting the minimal
requirements as enumerated in Section 39 of R.A. No. 9003, eventually
upgrade to sanitary landfill;

Ensure the full compliance of all of the barangays in the SMW ordinance;

Encourage the community to participate in SWM;

Give an ample budget to the SWM; and

v VvV VYV VY

Fully implement R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR for the optimum management
of the Municipality's solid waste.

Given the condition of the municipality, the acquisition of sanitary landfill
which entails huge amount of capital may deem impractical for LGU Labo. However,
we encourage the municipality to evaluate an alternative for the conventional
sanitary landfill such as adaptation of a Manual Sanitary Landfill. Also, the
municipality could collaborate with the nearby municipality for the construction of
shared sanitary landfill.

pertaining to salary deductions and adopt control measures by not permitting any
additional loan payments to be deducted on the salaries of those who are receiving
minimal net take home pay, to ensure compliance with the General Provisions of the
GAA for CY 2016.
